questions.q: What does 'Purna Swaraj' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Purna Swaraj' means complete or full independence.

questions.q: At which 1929 Congress session was Purna Swaraj declared the goal?
questions.explanation: The Congress declared Purna Swaraj as its goal at the Lahore session of 1929.

questions.q: On which date in 1930 was the first Independence Day observed to mark Purna Swaraj?
questions.explanation: The Congress called for the public observance of Independence Day on 26 January 1930.

questions.q: The date chosen to observe Purna Swaraj Day, 26 January, was later adopted as which national day?
questions.explanation: 26 January was later chosen as Republic Day, when the Constitution came into force in 1950.

questions.q: Purna Swaraj demanded freedom that was full, in contrast to which more limited goal?
questions.explanation: Purna Swaraj rejected mere Dominion status in favour of complete independence.

questions.q: Who presided over the Congress session that adopted the Purna Swaraj resolution?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru presided over the 1929 Lahore session that adopted Purna Swaraj.

questions.q: The Purna Swaraj declaration led directly into which mass movement of 1930?
questions.explanation: The Purna Swaraj declaration was followed by the Civil Disobedience Movement, launched with the Salt March in 1930.

questions.q: Purna Swaraj is a term combining 'Purna' with which word meaning self-rule?
questions.explanation: 'Swaraj' means self-rule, and 'Purna Swaraj' means complete self-rule.

questions.q: On Independence Day 1930, Indians across the country took a pledge to do what?
questions.explanation: People publicly read out a pledge committing themselves to the goal of complete independence.

questions.q: The Purna Swaraj resolution was passed by which organisation?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress adopted the Purna Swaraj resolution at Lahore.

questions.q: In which year did the Congress formally adopt Purna Swaraj as its goal?
questions.explanation: The Purna Swaraj resolution was adopted at the Lahore session in December 1929.

questions.q: At Lahore, the tricolour was hoisted as a pledge of Purna Swaraj on the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: Nehru hoisted the tricolour on the bank of the river Ravi at Lahore as a pledge of Purna Swaraj.

questions.q: Which earlier 1928 report had proposed Dominion status, a goal Purna Swaraj superseded?
questions.explanation: The 1928 Nehru Report sought Dominion status; when unmet, the Congress moved to Purna Swaraj in 1929.

questions.q: The younger leaders who pressed for Purna Swaraj over Dominion status included Nehru and which other figure?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru and Subhas Chandra Bose were the leading younger voices demanding complete independence.

questions.q: The Purna Swaraj resolution set a deadline that pushed the Congress toward action when the British did not concede what by end of 1929?
questions.explanation: The Congress had warned it would demand complete independence if Dominion status was not granted by the end of 1929.

questions.q: The text of the Purna Swaraj pledge read on 26 January 1930 condemned British rule as having done what to India?
options: Ruined India economically, politically, and culturally
questions.explanation: The pledge declared that British rule had economically, politically, culturally, and spiritually ruined India.

questions.q: Purna Swaraj as a demand grew louder after disappointment with which all-British 1928 body?
questions.explanation: The all-British Simon Commission's exclusion of Indians fuelled the demand for complete independence.

questions.q: Which veteran leader had used the word 'Swaraj' in his famous declaration before the Purna Swaraj demand?
questions.explanation: Tilak's cry 'Swaraj is my birthright' anticipated the later, fuller demand for Purna Swaraj.

questions.q: After the 1929 declaration, Independence Day was observed annually by nationalists until which year of actual freedom?
questions.explanation: Indians observed Purna Swaraj Day each 26 January until independence finally came in 1947.

questions.q: The drafting of the Purna Swaraj pledge for 26 January 1930 was largely the work of which leader?
questions.explanation: Gandhi drafted the Independence Day pledge that was read across the country on 26 January 1930.

questions.q: Purna Swaraj as a concept rejected the idea that India should remain within what relationship to Britain?
options: The British Empire as a dominion
questions.explanation: Purna Swaraj rejected remaining a dominion within the British Empire, demanding full sovereignty.

questions.q: On which exact date in December 1929 was the Purna Swaraj resolution adopted?
questions.explanation: The resolution was adopted on 19 December 1929, with the flag hoisted at midnight on 31 December 1929.

questions.q: The tricolour pledging Purna Swaraj was unfurled at Lahore at midnight ushering in which year?
questions.explanation: Nehru hoisted the flag at midnight on 31 December 1929, ushering in 1 January 1930.

questions.q: The Purna Swaraj resolution authorised the start of which programme to follow?
options: Civil disobedience including non-payment of taxes
questions.explanation: The resolution paved the way for civil disobedience, including non-payment of taxes, beginning in 1930.

questions.q: Which much earlier Congress session had first used the term 'swaraj' as a goal, decades before Purna Swaraj?
questions.explanation: The 1906 Calcutta session under Dadabhai Naoroji adopted 'swaraj' as a goal, foreshadowing the 1929 demand.

questions.q: The 26 January 1930 Independence Day pledge listed grievances under several heads, including which economic charge?
options: The 'drain' of India's wealth to Britain
questions.explanation: The pledge echoed the 'drain of wealth' critique, charging that British rule had impoverished India.

questions.q: Hasrat Mohani and Swami Kumaranand are credited with moving an early resolution for complete independence as far back as which year?
questions.explanation: Hasrat Mohani moved a resolution for complete independence at the Ahmedabad Congress in 1921, well before 1929.

questions.q: After Dominion status was not granted by end of 1929, the Congress empowered which committee to launch the next struggle?
options: The All India Congress Committee / Working Committee
questions.explanation: The Congress Working Committee was empowered to launch civil disobedience after the Purna Swaraj declaration.

questions.q: Although 26 January 1930 was the symbolic Independence Day, India's actual transfer of power came on which date?
questions.explanation: Actual independence came on 15 August 1947; 26 January was later honoured as Republic Day in 1950.

questions.q: The choice to commence the Constitution on 26 January 1950 was made specifically to honour what?
questions.explanation: 26 January 1950 was chosen for the Constitution's commencement to commemorate the 1930 Purna Swaraj Day.
